cards.title: Preconditioning scope and stabilization gate

citation.ieee_reference: [M5] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Preconditioning test," p. 15.
citation.title: Preconditioning test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, p. 15.

key_takeaways: The chapter frames preconditioning as the start-of-programme stability gate for new cells.
Capacity stability is judged on repeated discharges rather than a single initial cycle.
limitations: The chapter is short and assumes the standard-cycle block is already known.
allowed_deviations: Only manufacturer-specific rate details may be tailored after review.

cards.title: Preconditioning execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M5] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Preconditioning test," p. 15.
citation.title: Preconditioning test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, p. 15.

key_takeaways: The execution logic is charge-rest-discharge-rest repeated at least three times.
The cell temperature must remain within manufacturer limits during the repeated cycles.
limitations: The chapter does not add extra chemistry-specific safety rules.
allowed_deviations: Do not remove the 30 min rest framing without an explicit review note.

cards.title: Preconditioning stability decision

citation.ieee_reference: [M5] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Preconditioning test," p. 15.
citation.title: Preconditioning test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M5] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, p. 15.

key_takeaways: The stability gate is 2% change in subsequent discharge capacities.
The chapter allows extension to a maximum of 10 discharges if the cell is not yet stable.
limitations: The chapter does not prescribe a richer reporting format beyond the stability decision.
allowed_deviations: Changing the 2% gate or 10-cycle cap requires an explicit deviation note.

cards.title: Standard-cycle scope and planning role

citation.ieee_reference: [M6] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Standard cycle," pp. 16-17.
citation.title: Standard cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M6] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 16-17.

key_takeaways: The chapter defines the reference cycle used to follow capacity over time.
It also explains when C1 logic should give way to datasheet It logic for declared-capacity tracking.
limitations: The chapter assumes the user already understands the C1 versus It distinction.
allowed_deviations: Only the C1 versus It choice should move after review.

cards.title: Standard-cycle execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M6] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Standard cycle," pp. 16-17.
citation.title: Standard cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M6] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 16-17.

key_takeaways: The execution flow is discharge, CC-CV charge, rest, discharge with CV completion, rest, then final recharge.
The chapter keeps the C/25 taper explicit in both charge and discharge logic.
limitations: The chapter does not provide a universal rule for when It should replace C1; that still depends on the declared-capacity question.
allowed_deviations: Do not remove the CV completion or rest blocks without an explicit review note.

cards.title: Standard-cycle outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M6] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Standard cycle," pp. 16-17.
citation.title: Standard cycle
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M6] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 16-17.

key_takeaways: The chapter treats the standard cycle as a reference block for follow-up over the cell lifetime.
The output must preserve whether the reported result follows C1 capacity or datasheet-rated-capacity logic.
limitations: The chapter does not itself define a full report template.
allowed_deviations: If the reporting label changes, preserve the actual execution logic that was used.
